title: Structure-Aware Conditional VAE for Multi-Objective Therapeutic Peptide Design
description: A deep generative model with cross-attention fusion between sequence and 3D structure modalities that jointly optimizes peptide sequences for structural validity and pharmacokinetic properties. Uses a stabilized disentangled latent space (structure, property, sequence factors) to enable precise property-conditioned generation while addressing posterior collapse through cyclical annealing and free bits.
motivation: Therapeutic peptides have high clinical potential but >90% fail due to poor pharmacokinetic properties. Recent methods like PepMorph (CVAE for morphology), PLUM (disentangled VAE for antimicrobial peptides), and MMCD (multimodal diffusion) have explored related directions, but no method combines: (1) cross-attention fusion of sequence and 3D structure, (2) disentangled latent spaces for PK properties, and (3) stabilized training to prevent posterior collapse. This work addresses the gap for end-to-end joint optimization with explicit controllability.
proposed_approach: StruCVAE-Pep (Structure-Conditioned Variational Autoencoder) consists of: (1) Multimodal encoder with cross-attention fusion between ESM-2 sequence representations and DMPNN structure encodings; (2) Disentangled latent space with separate factors for structure (z_s), properties (z_p), and sequence (z_seq); (3) Conditional autoregressive decoder generating sequences from property-conditioned latents; (4) Training stabilization via cyclical KL annealing, free bits, and aggressive encoder updates to prevent posterior collapse. Trained with composite loss: reconstruction + KL divergence + property prediction + structure contrastive + adversarial disentanglement losses.
key_innovations: Cross-attention fusion for early interaction between sequence and 3D structure modalities, improving over late fusion (MMCD, Multi-Peptide)
Stabilized disentangled VAE training using cyclical annealing + free bits + aggressive encoder optimization to prevent posterior collapse
Explicit disentanglement of structure, property, and sequence factors enabling independent control over PK properties
Joint gradient-based optimization of structure validity and pharmacokinetic properties in a unified framework

related_work.contribution: Structure-based protein sequence design using message-passing neural networks
related_work.limitation: Only optimizes for structure fit; no pharmacokinetic property guidance
related_work.our_difference: We jointly optimize structure AND PK properties with end-to-end gradients and disentangled control

related_work.contribution: Diffusion-based backbone generation for proteins and cyclic peptides
related_work.limitation: Generates backbones only; requires separate sequence design and property filtering
related_work.our_difference: Single model generates sequences conditioned on both structure and property targets with cross-modal fusion

related_work.paper: Liu et al. (2025) - Benchmarking cyclic peptide permeability prediction
related_work.contribution: Comprehensive benchmark of 13 ML models; DMPNN achieves R² ≈ 0.65 on random split
related_work.finding: Graph-based models (DMPNN) achieve best performance; regression > classification
related_work.our_build_upon: We use DMPNN as our structure encoder and extend to generative setting with cross-attention fusion

related_work.contribution: Graph neural network for cell-penetrating peptide classification (SOTA)
related_work.limitation: Classification only; does not generate new peptides
related_work.our_difference: We integrate graph-based property prediction into a generative model with explicit structure encoding

related_work.contribution: Multi-objective peptide generation with masked diffusion and MCTS guidance
related_work.limitation: Sequence-only; does not incorporate 3D structure information
related_work.our_difference: Structure-aware generation using explicit 3D conformation encoding via cross-attention

related_work.contribution: Structure-aware protein language model using Foldseek 3Di tokens
related_work.limitation: Representation learning for prediction tasks, not generation
related_work.our_difference: Apply structure-aware representations to conditional peptide generation with cross-modal fusion

related_work.contribution: Transformer-based CVAE with masking mechanism for morphology-aware peptide generation
related_work.limitation: Sequence-only with descriptor conditioning; no explicit 3D structure integration
related_work.our_difference: We add explicit 3D structure encoder with cross-attention fusion and target PK properties (permeability, stability) rather than aggregation morphology

related_work.contribution: Disentangled VAE with Zseq/Zfunc/Zlength latents for antimicrobial peptides
related_work.our_difference: We add explicit 3D structure encoding via DMPNN and cross-attention fusion; target therapeutic PK properties vs antimicrobial activity

related_work.contribution: Multi-modal contrastive diffusion for peptide sequence-structure co-generation
related_work.limitation: Late fusion via contrastive alignment; no explicit disentanglement of structure vs property factors
related_work.our_difference: Early fusion via cross-attention; explicit disentangled latent space with stabilized training

related_work.contribution: PeptideBERT + GNN with contrastive learning for peptide property prediction
related_work.limitation: Prediction-only task; late fusion of pre-trained encoders
related_work.our_difference: Generative model with early cross-attention fusion; end-to-end trainable

related_work.venue: Journal of Chemical Information and Modeling
related_work.contribution: First comprehensive database of 7,334 cyclic peptides with membrane permeability
related_work.our_use: Primary training dataset for our model

hypothesis: Integrating 3D structure information via cross-attention fusion into a stabilized disentangled VAE will: (H1) Improve permeability prediction accuracy achieving R² > 0.70 (vs. R² ≈ 0.65 for DMPNN baseline) by capturing conformational determinants of membrane permeation; (H2) Enable controllable generation targeting specific permeability/stability values within 0.5 log units through disentangled property latents; (H3) Produce 2-3x more 'drug-like' peptides (simultaneously permeable, stable, soluble) compared to sequential generate-then-filter pipelines; (H4) Achieve <20% posterior collapse rate using cyclical annealing + free bits vs >50% for vanilla disentangled VAE.

confirming: Permeability prediction R² > 0.70 on held-out test (vs. R² ≈ 0.65 for DMPNN baseline)
Property-conditioned generation achieves MAE < 0.5 log Papp units from target
>30% of generated peptides pass all three PK filters (permeable, stable, soluble)
>80% of generated sequences achieve pLDDT > 70 in AlphaFold2 validation
Cross-attention fusion shows >5% improvement over late fusion ablation
<20% of latent dimensions collapse during training (KL < 0.1)
refuting: Structure vs. sequence ablation shows <3% improvement in property prediction
Disentangled latents collapse (>50% inactive) or fail to provide independent property control
Property conditioning ineffective (variance similar to unconditional generation)
Mode collapse: <50% of generated sequences structurally designable
Cross-attention provides no benefit over late fusion (<2% difference)

baselines: DMPNN: Best-performing baseline from Liu et al. 2025 (R² ≈ 0.65)
PepMorph: Transformer CVAE with masking (reimplemented for PK)
PLUM: Disentangled VAE adapted for PK properties
experiments: Property prediction benchmarking vs. DMPNN and other methods from Liu et al. 2025
Conditional generation with varying permeability targets
Ablation studies: without structure encoder, without disentanglement, without cross-attention, without stabilization
Structure validation via AlphaFold2 pLDDT scoring

training_stabilization.problem: Disentangled VAEs suffer from posterior collapse where latent dimensions become inactive
solutions: Cyclical KL annealing (Fu et al. 2019): Gradually increase β from 0 to 1 over multiple cycles
Free bits (Kingma et al. 2016): Minimum KL threshold per dimension to prevent collapse
Aggressive encoder training (He et al. 2019): Multiple encoder updates per decoder update when encoder lags
Decoder weakening: Word dropout to force reliance on latent variables
β-VAE regularization: β > 1 for property latent to encourage disentanglement
training_stabilization.target: <20% collapsed dimensions vs >50% for vanilla disentangled VAE

novelty: While PepMorph uses transformer CVAEs and PLUM uses disentangled VAEs for peptides, StruCVAE-Pep uniquely combines: (1) cross-attention fusion of sequence and 3D structure for early multimodal interaction (vs late fusion in MMCD/Multi-Peptide), (2) disentangled latent space for PK properties, and (3) systematic stabilization techniques (cyclical annealing + free bits + aggressive encoder) to prevent posterior collapse in disentangled peptide VAEs.
expected_impact: Scientific: Demonstrates that cross-attention fusion outperforms late fusion for multimodal peptide generation; provides recipe for training stable disentangled VAEs on biological sequences
Practical: Reduces experimental screening burden by 10-100x through in silico multi-objective optimization with controllable property targeting
Clinical: Addresses the key barrier (permeability) preventing oral peptide therapeutics
